title: Single-FOV MERFISH Image Decoding And Cell Segmentation
summary: Run the full MERFISH image-analysis pipeline on one field of view of U2OS cells: decode transcripts against a 130-gene MHD4 codebook, segment nuclei from the DAPI tile, assign transcripts to cells, and emit a cell-by-gene count matrix plus quality metrics.
taskPrompt:
You are working on an Ubuntu VM to run the full MERFISH image-analysis pipeline
for one field of view of a human U2OS cell population stained against a
130-gene panel.

Task directory:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/merfish_image_decoding_segmentation_1/base`

Inputs (all under `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/merfish_image_decoding_segmentation_1/base/input`; treat as read-only):
- `experiment.json` plus `primary_images.json`, `nuclei.json` and the per-FOV
  manifests `primary_images-fov_000.json` / `nuclei-fov_000.json`. These
  describe a starfish Experiment with one FOV, 8 hybridization rounds, and
  2 color channels.
- 16 primary fluorescence TIFFs
  `primary_images-fov_000-Z0-H<h>-C<c>.tiff` (h in 0..7, c in 0..1); each is
  2048x2048.
- 1 DAPI nuclear stain TIFF `nuclei-fov_000-Z0-H0-C0.tiff`.
- `codebook.json` mapping 130 target genes and 10 Blank controls to 16-bit
  MHD4 (Hamming-distance-4 with 1-bit error correction) binary codewords.
- `experiment.json` `extras.scale_factors` gives per-(round, channel)
  normalization scalars.
- `output_contract.json` lists the exact output files, columns, and dtypes
  your pipeline must produce.
- `runtime_env/pyproject.toml`, `runtime_env/uv.lock`, and
  `runtime_env/python-version` pin the Python 3.11 environment that
  starfish 0.3.4 and Cellpose 3.1.1 need. The canonical agent-facing entry
  point `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/merfish_image_decoding_segmentation_1/base/software/merfish_runtime.sh`
  behaves like a Python interpreter, keeps its `uv` cache and resolved
  environment under `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/merfish_image_decoding_segmentation_1/base/output`,
  and uses `runtime_env/pyproject.toml` (with the staged `uv.lock`) as the
  frozen runtime manifest. You can invoke it directly
  (`software/merfish_runtime.sh your_script.py`) or materialize the env
  yourself with `uv sync --frozen --project input/runtime_env` and then run
  the resulting `.venv/bin/python`.

Recommended workflow:
1. Load the starfish Experiment and sanity-check the image and codebook.
2. Preprocess: high-pass filter or background-subtract, normalize intensity
   using the provided `scale_factors`, and confirm the pre-registered tiles
   are still aligned.
3. Run starfish BlobDetector on each round/channel and extract 16-bit
   intensity traces per spot.
4. Decode with an MHD4-aware decoder (starfish `CheckAll`) with 1-bit error
   correction; filter by magnitude and distance thresholds.
5. Run Cellpose on the DAPI image with a pretrained nuclei model and a
   diameter appropriate for U2OS (roughly 30-50 px).
6. Assign decoded transcripts to the nearest segmented cell; mark transcripts
   outside every cell as extracellular and exclude them from the
   cell-by-gene matrix.
7. Build the cell-by-gene count matrix for the 130 real genes (exclude
   blanks) and compute the quality metrics listed in `output_contract.json`.

Outputs must go into `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/merfish_image_decoding_segmentation_1/base/output`:
- `decoded_transcripts.csv` with columns `gene, x, y, is_exact, total_magnitude`.
- `segmentation.tiff` - 2048x2048 `uint16` labeled mask (background=0).
- `cell_by_gene.csv` - leading `cell_id` column followed by the 130 real genes
  in codebook order; integer counts.
- `quality_metrics.json` - scalar keys `total_decoded_transcripts`,
  `blank_rate`, `exact_match_fraction`, `n_cells`, `assigned_fraction`,
  `mean_transcripts_per_cell`.

All coordinates (`x`, `y`) in `decoded_transcripts.csv` must be pixel
coordinates within the 2048x2048 FOV so they line up with the segmentation
mask.
agentMustDo: Invoke the canonical Python entry point `software/merfish_runtime.sh` (or materialize the env with `uv sync --frozen --project input/runtime_env/`).
Load the staged starfish experiment (16 primary TIFFs + 1 DAPI TIFF + manifests + codebook) and sanity-check alignment.
Preprocess images: high-pass / background subtract, apply per-(round, channel) `scale_factors`, confirm registration.
Detect fluorescent spots with starfish `BlobDetector` and decode with the MHD4-aware `CheckAll` decoder plus 1-bit error correction.
Segment nuclei from the DAPI tile using Cellpose 3.1.1 with the `nuclei` model and a diameter around 30-50 px for U2OS cells.
Assign decoded transcripts to the nearest labeled cell and mark out-of-cell transcripts as extracellular.
Emit `decoded_transcripts.csv`, `segmentation.tiff`, `cell_by_gene.csv`, and `quality_metrics.json` under `output/` in pixel coordinates.

inputFiles.name: Starfish experiment manifest
inputFiles.description: Top-level starfish Experiment v5.0.0 manifest with per-(round, channel) `scale_factors`.

inputFiles.name: MHD4 codebook
inputFiles.description: 140 targets (130 real genes + 10 `Blank-*` controls) with 16-bit MHD4 codewords.

inputFiles.name: Primary images manifest
inputFiles.description: Starfish top-level primary-images manifest trimmed to `fov_000` only.

inputFiles.name: Nuclei images manifest
inputFiles.description: Starfish top-level nuclei manifest trimmed to `fov_000` only.

inputFiles.name: Primary FOV tile manifest
inputFiles.description: Per-FOV manifest enumerating 16 primary tiles for (round 0-7, channel 0-1).

inputFiles.name: Nuclei FOV tile manifest
inputFiles.description: Per-FOV manifest for the single DAPI tile.

inputFiles.name: Primary fluorescence tiles
inputFiles.description: 16 pre-registered 2048x2048 tiles (h in 0..7, c in 0..1) to decode against the codebook.

inputFiles.name: DAPI nuclear stain tile
inputFiles.description: Single 2048x2048 DAPI image used to segment nuclei.

inputFiles.name: Task brief
inputFiles.description: Evaluator-safe solve-time brief (no thresholds, no gold reference).

inputFiles.name: Output contract
inputFiles.description: Required output paths, columns, and dtypes; no per-gene expected counts.

inputFiles.name: Runtime dependency lockfile
inputFiles.description: Resolved uv lockfile (87 packages against Python 3.11) consumed by `uv sync --frozen` and the `software/merfish_runtime.sh` wrapper.

inputFiles.name: Canonical Python entry point
inputFiles.description: Wrapper that behaves like a Python interpreter; forwards args to `uv run --frozen --project input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml python "$@"` with the agent-scoped uv env under `output/.agent_runtime_env/`.

referenceFiles.name: Decoded transcripts
referenceFiles.description: Columns `gene, x, y, is_exact, total_magnitude`; one row per threshold-filtered decoded spot in pixel coordinates.

referenceFiles.name: Cell segmentation mask
referenceFiles.description: 2048x2048 `uint16` labeled mask with background=0 and contiguous positive cell labels.

referenceFiles.name: Cell-by-gene count matrix
referenceFiles.description: Leading `cell_id` column followed by the 130 real genes in codebook order; integer counts.

evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions** (auto score=0 if triggered):
- Missing any of the four required output files.
- `decoded_transcripts.csv` unreadable, missing any of `gene, x, y, is_exact, total_magnitude`, or having zero rows.
- `segmentation.tiff` not an integer-dtype `(2048, 2048)` array.
- `cell_by_gene.csv` columns not `['cell_id', *130 real genes in codebook order]`.
- `quality_metrics.json` missing any of the six required scalar keys.

**Decoding credit gate**: if per-gene count Pearson `r < 0.50`, all decoding-derived credit (Pearson + total-count + blank-rate + spatial, combined weight `0.65`) is zeroed to prevent random-label submissions from earning partial credit on total count alone.

**Weighted scoring** (sum clipped to `[0.0, 1.0]`):
- Pearson per-gene correlation: `0.30` (full at `r >= 0.90`, half at `r in [0.70, 0.90)`, `0.06` at `r in [0.50, 0.70)`).
- Total-count plausibility: `0.10` full credit when `|total - 42,212| / 42,212 <= 0.20`.
- Blank-rate gate: `0.10` full credit at `blank_rate <= 0.05`, half credit at `blank_rate in (0.05, 0.07]`.
- Spatial concordance: `0.15` full credit when at least `80%` of decoded transcripts match a reference transcript of the same gene within 3 px, half at `[60%, 80%)`.
- Segmented cell count: `0.10` full credit when `50 <= n_cells <= 500`.
- Assigned fraction: `0.10` full credit when cell count is in range and `assigned_fraction >= 0.50`.
- Mean transcripts per cell: `0.05` full credit when cell count is in range and `50 <= mean_transcripts_per_cell <= 1000`.
- Matrix consistency: `0.10` full credit when `cell_by_gene.csv` has exactly `n_cells` rows and column sums tie out to the assigned-transcript counts.

**Current fixture expectations**
- Positive fixture: `1.0`
- Negative fixture: `0.0` (random gene labels fail the decoding credit gate; empty segmentation fails structural gates)

classifier.rationale: The task involves image analysis and decoding in a biological context, specifically related to cell imaging.
